title: Benzoylacetanilide as a Gravimetric Reagent for Uranium(Vl)
author: SANAT KUMAR MANDAL
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Burdwan University, Burdwan West Bengal 

  Manuscript received 18 January 1973; accepted 11 May 1973  

 Uranium(VI) has been determined gravimetrically with benzoylacetanilide. The orange-red complex, UO 2 (C 15 H 12 NO 2 ) 2 , 2H 2 O, is precipitated in the pH-range 6.0-6.6, adjusted with pyridine and is weighed as such, after drying at 110 0  Separation of UO 2  2 + from Co 2 +, Ni 2 + Pb 2 +, BP+, AI 3  Fe 3+ , Cr 3+ , Ce 2 +, Th 4 +, VO 2 + or MoO 4  2-  has been effected by precipitating the complex in presencce of magnesium-EDTA, while sulphosalicylic acid has been used to mask Be 2+  or Zr 4 +. The reliative standard deviation is 0.04% and the mean relative error is ± 0.14%. The uranium content of some samples of its ores have also been determined.
